name: Western New York Independent Living, Inc. (WNYIL)
summary: The anchor Center for Independent Living for the Buffalo/Erie area and lead of a family of disability-run agencies serving Western New York.
source_notes: First-party site. Anchor partner (§3). disabled_led=true rests on the federal Center for Independent Living definition: CILs are consumer-controlled (>=51% of staff/board are people with disabilities). Address confirmed on the NYSILC ILC directory. NEEDS OWNER/ORG SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).

name: Independent Living of Niagara County (ILNC)
summary: A peer-run independent living center serving people with disabilities across Niagara County.
source_notes: First-party WNYIL family-of-agencies page. Peer-run ILC; disabled_led per CIL consumer-control federal requirement. Niagara County (adjacent to the Erie beachhead). NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Independent Living of the Genesee Region (ILGR)
summary: A peer-directed disability civil-rights organization serving Genesee, Wyoming, and Orleans Counties (also has an Albion office at 243 South Main Street, Albion 14411).
source_notes: First-party WNYIL page. Self-describes as 'a grassroots, peer directed, multi-cultural civil rights organization for/of people with disabilities.' disabled_led per CIL consumer-control. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Mental Health Peer Connection (MHPC)
summary: A peer-run behavioral-health organization providing advocacy, peer counseling, and independent-living support, run for and by people with behavioral health disabilities.
source_notes: First-party WNYIL page. Explicitly 'an organization run for and by people with behavioral health disabilities' — disabled_led directly stated. WNYIL affiliate; shares the 3108 Main St address. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Southwestern Independent Living Center, Inc. (SILC)
summary: A nonresidential nonprofit Center for Independent Living serving people with disabilities throughout Chautauqua County.
source_notes: NYSILC official ILC directory + first-party site (silcchq.org). Designated NYS CIL, consumer-controlled per federal definition. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: Directions in Independent Living (DIL)
summary: A Center for Independent Living serving Cattaraugus and Allegany Counties, with a satellite office in Wellsville (55 E. Fassett Lane, Wellsville 14895).
source_notes: First-party site + NYSILC ILC directory. Designated NYS CIL, consumer-controlled per federal definition. NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

name: New York Association on Independent Living (NYAIL)
summary: The statewide membership association of New York's Independent Living Centers, advocating for the independent living and rights of New Yorkers with disabilities.
source_notes: First-party site. STATEWIDE ANCHOR — reference/context only, not WNY-local; do not seed deep. disabled_led leans on its CIL-membership structure; the About page does not itself assert a disability-majority board — CONFIRM before publishing that flag.

name: People Inc. Health Services Building (with ECMC Center for Dental Care)
summary: A health and dental center in North Buffalo built around serving people with developmental disabilities, with a dental wheelchair-tilt lift and abundant accessible parking.
source_notes: Developer (Uniland) page + People Inc. corroboration. Accessible-parking evidence exists ('abundant patient parking and a designated drop-off under a porte-cochere') BUT the schema's accessible_parking attribute is place-only (applies_to_kind='place'), so it cannot attach to this provider record — recorded here as a note pending the schema fix in gaps.md (make accessible_parking apply to both kinds). Co-located ECMC dental clinic serves people with developmental disabilities and has a wheelchair-tilt lift (dental positioning, NOT a medical exam table — no key). Secondary source; recruit for MDE photo-verification.

name: ECMC Center for Dental Care — Special Needs Dentistry
summary: Erie County Medical Center's special-needs dental program serving people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, stroke/TBI, and dementia, in-clinic or under general anesthesia.
source_notes: ECMC (public hospital) program page states it welcomes individuals with intellectual/developmental disabilities, stroke/TBI history, and dementia, and offers full-mouth rehab under general anesthesia. Disability-competent care explicitly stated; no MDE/exam-table claim — assert none.

name: Jericho Road Community Health Center — Doat Street Clinic
summary: A Buffalo FQHC clinic location listed as handicap accessible with accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking.
source_notes: 211WNY (United Way) directory: 'Accessible entrance, restrooms, and parking spaces.' FQHC — HHS-funded, relevant to the July 8 2026 MDE deadline. NOTE: the source also states accessible PARKING, but the schema's accessible_parking attribute is place-only (applies_to_kind='place') and cannot attach to a provider — held as a note pending the schema fix in gaps.md. Physical-access fields are directory-reported, not disability-specific care; disability_literate left false. Strong recruit; verify MDE (exam table, scale) via photo.

name: Neighborhood Health Center — Northwest
summary: A Buffalo FQHC offering primary, dental, and specialty care, listed as handicap accessible.
source_notes: 211WNY directory. FQHC (HHS-funded — July 8 2026 MDE deadline). Only a general 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' flag published. disability_literate false; recruit for MDE verification.

name: Neighborhood Health Center — Blasdell
summary: A suburban Erie County FQHC location listed as handicap accessible.
source_notes: 211WNY directory. FQHC (HHS-funded — MDE deadline). General handicap-accessible flag only; mapped conservatively. disability_literate false; recruit for MDE verification.

name: Aspire of WNY — Health Care Services (Article 28 Clinic)
summary: An Article 28 medical clinic within a longstanding developmental-disability agency, offering general medical, PT, speech, and seating/positioning services and listed as handicap accessible.
source_notes: 211WNY directory + Aspire site. Aspire (formerly Cerebral Palsy Association of WNY) serves people with developmental disabilities; clinic lists seating/positioning aids, mobility training, AAC, and general medical care — supports disability_literate. Only a general accessible flag; MDE needs photo verification. Address returns as Buffalo 14225 in one field, Cheektowaga in another (14225 is the Cheektowaga ZIP).

name: Evergreen Health
summary: A Buffalo FQHC providing primary, behavioral, and supportive care with a stated non-discrimination policy and disability accommodations including sign-language interpreters.
source_notes: Evergreen's own accessibility/non-discrimination page provides qualified sign-language interpreters and non-discrimination on the basis of disability. disability_literate grounded in communication-access accommodation, NOT any physical-MDE claim (page states no exam-table/scale/entrance detail). FQHC (HHS-funded — MDE deadline). Recruit for physical MDE verification.

name: VIA — Visually Impaired Advancement (Low Vision Clinic)
summary: A Buffalo agency and low-vision clinic providing vision rehabilitation and clinical services for people who are blind or visually impaired; listed as handicap accessible.
source_notes: 211WNY directory. VIA serves people who are legally blind or visually impaired with low-vision rehabilitation — supports disability_literate (sensory-disability competence). Only a general accessible flag; physical MDE not addressed.

name: Erie County Medical Center (ECMC) — Main Campus
summary: Erie County's public hospital and Level 1 trauma center, recognized for safe patient handling and mobility; a candidate to recruit for documented accessible diagnostic equipment.
source_notes: Public hospital. Recognized by the Association of Safe Patient Handling Professionals (secondary mention) and hosts the special-needs dental program (separate record). No published disability-specific-care or MDE statement on general pages, so disability_literate=false — a recruit tied to the ADA Title II state/local MDE deadline (Aug 9 2026). Verify all attributes before asserting.

name: University at Buffalo — Special Care Dental Clinic (Squire Hall)
street: 3435 Main Street (Squire Hall, UB South Campus)
summary: A UB dental clinic (state-funded, projected to open 2027) purpose-built for people with intellectual/developmental disabilities, featuring a wheelchair tilt, wheelchair-accessible panoramic X-ray, sensory-friendly rooms, and extra-large restrooms.
source_notes: UB News (institutional). Explicitly for individuals with intellectual/developmental disabilities; features a wheelchair tilt, wheelchair-accessible panoramic X-ray, sensory-friendly rooms. IMPORTANT: projected to open 2027 — PLANNED, not operational. Do not surface as available. Strong future disability-competent listing.

name: University at Buffalo / Arc GLOW — S-Miles To Go Mobile Dental Program
street: Arc of Genesee Orleans Community Center, 38 Woodrow Road
summary: A UB-partnered mobile dental clinic serving WNY patients with disabilities from a wheelchair-lift-equipped van, with overflow care at UB's Squire Hall clinic in Erie County.
source_notes: UB News. A 42-foot, three-chair dental van with digital X-ray, sterilization center, and wheelchair lift; UB partnered with Arc of Genesee Orleans to serve patients with disabilities. GEOGRAPHY: home base is Batavia (Genesee County), NOT Erie — included as a WNY disability-dental program that also delivers care at UB's Erie County clinic. Van wheelchair lift is a transport lift, not a facility MDE attribute (note only).

name: Catholic Health (WNY)
summary: A Buffalo-based nonprofit health system offering 24/7 video remote interpreting including ASL across its network; a candidate to recruit for documented accessible diagnostic equipment.
source_notes: PR Newswire (system press release). Uses InDemand video remote interpreting including American Sign Language — supports disability_literate on the Deaf/HoH communication-access dimension only. No physical-MDE statement; recruit for exam-table/scale/entrance verification (Aug 9 2026 deadline). PRESS-RELEASE source — corroborate on Catholic Health's own site before elevating.

name: Service Bridges, Inc.
summary: A Deaf-owned ASL and sign-language interpreting agency serving the Deaf, DeafBlind, and hard-of-hearing community.
source_notes: Business's own About page states it was founded to be an agency 'run by the Deaf'; founder/CEO described as a Deaf man. Also appears in Gallaudet University's Apple Maps 'Deaf-Owned Businesses in Western NY' guide. High reliability for the disabled-owned/led claim (first-party + third-party curated list). disability_literate true (interpreting for the Deaf community is the core service). NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING (no proof).
